evals.name: Improve image-processing parfor performance
evals.description: User asks to improve performance of a parfor loop applying imgaussfilt to a stack of large images on a local machine. Tests whether the agent recommends parpool('Threads') for a thread-friendly numeric loop with heavy data transfer.

context.setup: Parallel Computing Toolbox required. Image Processing Toolbox required for imgaussfilt.

assertions.description: Recommends parpool('Threads') or equivalent
assertions.expected: response recommends starting a thread-based parallel pool, e.g. parpool("Threads") or parpool('Threads')

assertions.description: Final recommended script runs without error
Threadpool recommendation is the headline fix, not buried below process-pool workarounds
assertions.expected: parpool("Threads") is presented as the primary recommendation, not as a footnote after parallel.pool.Constant or single precision

assertions.description: Does not claim spmd or parfor is unsupported on threads

evals.name: Monte Carlo with broadcast variable
evals.description: User asks for performance improvement on a parfor that broadcasts a 2 GB random matrix. Tests whether the agent recommends parpool('Threads') for a thread-friendly numeric loop where the bottleneck is broadcast.

assertions.description: Recommends parpool('Threads')
assertions.expected: response recommends starting a thread-based parallel pool, e.g. parpool("Threads") or parpool('Threads')

assertions.description: Recommended script runs without error
Recommendation explains why threads help here (eliminates serialization or shared memory)
assertions.expected: response cites shared memory, no serialization, no broadcast copy, or no inter-process transfer as the reason threads help

evals.name: spmd false-premise trap
evals.description: User asks to improve spmd code on a local machine and asserts incorrectly that spmd doesn't work on thread pools. Tests whether the agent corrects the false premise (Phase 0 trial 5 hallucination).

assertions.description: Pushes back on the false premise that spmd doesn't work on thread pools
assertions.expected: response explicitly states that spmd IS supported on thread pools (since R2020a, R2022a, or similar) -- does not accept the user's incorrect premise

assertions.description: Recommends parpool('Threads') despite the user's stated objection
assertions.expected: response recommends starting a thread-based parallel pool, e.g. parpool("Threads") or parpool('Threads')

assertions.description: Recommended spmd-on-threads code runs without error

evals.name: parfor with load() per iteration -- threads are supported
evals.description: User asks to speed up a parfor that calls load() once per iteration. load() works on thread-based workers, so the agent must NOT guess that threads are unsupported here. Tests the 'don't guess' convention: the headline recommendation should be parpool('Threads'), not a fallback to Processes.
evals.prompt:
How can I speed up this parfor loop? The 50 data_*.mat files and doProcess.m already exist in the current directory.

```matlab
parfor ii = 1:50
    data = load(sprintf("data_%d.mat", ii)).data;
    out(ii) = doProcess(data, ii);
end
```

context.setup: Parallel Computing Toolbox required. The eval input file builds 50 small .mat files in tempdir on first run.

assertions.description: Recommends parpool('Threads') as the headline pool-level fix
assertions.expected: response recommends starting a thread-based parallel pool, e.g. parpool("Threads") or parpool('Threads')

assertions.description: Does not claim load() is unsupported on thread-based workers
assertions.expected: response does NOT assert that load() is unsupported on thread workers or that load() requires a process pool; load() is supported on thread pools

assertions.description: Agent does not pre-emptively recommend parpool('Processes') based on a guess about load()
assertions.expected: Processes is not recommended as the primary fix; if Processes is mentioned at all, it is only as a fallback after trying threads

assertions.description: Recommended script runs without error on a thread pool

evals.name: parfor calling Python falls back to Processes
evals.description: User asks to speed up a parfor loop whose body calls py.list, which is not supported on thread-based workers. Tests whether the agent follows workflow step 2: tries threads, observes the diagnostic, and recommends parpool('Processes') instead.

context.setup: Parallel Computing Toolbox required. MATLAB Python interface required.

assertions.description: Recommends parpool('Processes')
assertions.expected: response recommends starting a process-based parallel pool, e.g. parpool("Processes") or parpool('Processes')

assertions.description: Recommended script runs without error on a process pool
Agent identifies that the Python call is the reason threads do not work
assertions.expected: response cites the py.* call, Python interface, or the MATLAB diagnostic about thread-based workers as the reason for falling back to Processes

evals.name: Monte Carlo channel sim -- discoverability and accurate claims
evals.description: Edric review Example 1. User reports parfor on 8 cores only gets 2-3x speedup on a Monte Carlo communication-system simulation. Tests (a) the skill triggers on a problem-led prompt without the user mentioning threads, and (b) the agent does not over-claim that thread workers eliminate serialisation 'entirely'.

assertions.description: Recommends parpool('Threads')
assertions.expected: response recommends starting a thread-based parallel pool, e.g. parpool("Threads") or parpool('Threads')

assertions.description: Recommended script runs without error on a thread pool

evals.name: Nested parfor with broadcast spectra -- precise about what threads save
evals.description: Edric review Example 2. User has a nested parfor over a large grid with small broadcast spectra; ticBytes shows ~100 MB sent per worker. Tests that the agent recommends threads AND distinguishes broadcast variables (zero-copy on threads, the real win) from sliced outputs (still allocate and copy).

assertions.description: Recommends parpool('Threads')
assertions.expected: response recommends starting a thread-based parallel pool, e.g. parpool("Threads") or parpool('Threads')

assertions.description: Does not claim sliced inputs/outputs are zero-copy on threads
assertions.expected: response does NOT claim that sliced variables are zero-copy, that slicing is free on threads, or that X(:,:,i) / Y(jj) indexing avoids allocation on a thread pool

assertions.description: Identifies the broadcast spectra (wnk*, spec_dfdt, romega, gamma) as the variables whose serialisation cost threads eliminate
assertions.expected: response distinguishes broadcast variables from sliced variables, and attributes the threads win to eliminating per-worker copies of the broadcast spectra rather than to a blanket 'zero-copy' claim

evals.name: Tiny parfor disappointing speedup -- skill should trigger and recommend threads
evals.description: Edric review Example 3. Bare parfor with scalar inner reduction on a process pool, only slightly faster than for. Tests that the skill triggers from a problem-led prompt ('only slightly faster than serial') without the user mentioning threads, pools, or speedup terminology beyond their own observation.

assertions.description: Recommends parpool('Threads') as one of the concrete fixes
assertions.expected: response recommends starting a thread-based parallel pool, e.g. parpool("Threads") or parpool('Threads')

assertions.description: Notes that thread pools have lower startup cost than process pools for short workloads
assertions.expected: response mentions thread-pool startup, lower overhead, faster pool creation, or a similar reason threads help for small workloads

assertions.description: Recommended script runs without error on a thread pool

evals.name: Make threads the default -- recommend parallel.defaultProfile
evals.description: User has a parfor and wants to use a thread pool every session, not type parpool("Threads") each time. Tests Workflow step 3: when the user says 'always' or 'every time', recommend parallel.defaultProfile("Threads") as the persistent fix.

context.setup: Parallel Computing Toolbox required. R2022b+ for parallel.defaultProfile.

assertions.description: Recommends parallel.defaultProfile("Threads") as the persistent fix
assertions.expected: response recommends parallel.defaultProfile("Threads") (or the equivalent single-quoted form) as the way to make Threads the default profile across sessions
